ndation.md\nprompts/60-composition-job-orchestration.md\nprompts/96-compose-dev-and-prod-shapes.md\nprompts/49-beat-sheet-refinement-ui-and-chat.md\nprompts/93-accessibility-pass-and-keyboard-support.md\nprompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\nprompts/47-character-refinement-via-chat.md\nprompts/16-asset-metadata-and-file-records.md\nprompts/76-audio-preview-and-segment-status-ui.md\nprompts/53-outline-drill-down.md\nprompts/66-rewrite-prior-segments.md\nprompts/18-postgres-job-runner-skeleton.md\nprompts/43-brief-normalization-service.md\nprompts/base_prompt.md\nprompts/50-story-length-duration-and-chapter-setup.md\nprompts/86-completed-session-summary-and-search.md\nprompts/82-html-markdown-and-reader-formatting.md\nprompts/77-final-audio-assembly.md\nprompts/79-audio-pipeline-tests.md\nprompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\nprompts/89-full-journey-e2e-test.md\nprompts/57-composition-prompt-assembly.md\nprompts/39-bridge-and-replay-tests.md\nprompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.md\nprompts/31-intent-parser-service.md\nprompts/75-audio-job-orchestration.md\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md\nprompts/85-audio-player-with-text-sync-hooks.md\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.md\nprompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\nprompts/08-repo-quality-tooling.md\nprompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.md\nprompts/73-gemini-tts-integration.md\nprompts/71-audio-length-estimation.md\nprompts/22-workspace-two-pane-layout.md\nprompts/38-session-replay-and-resume-hydration.md\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.md\nprompts/40-genre-selection-api-and-ui.md\nprompts/70-audio-settings-model-and-ui.md\nprompts/69-composition-loop-e2e-test.md\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines\nprompts/27-reusable-form-and-card-components.md\nprompts/98-demo-data-and-onboarding.md\nprompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.md\nprompts/51-correlation-heuristics.md\nprompts/35-chat-commands-and-quick-actions.md\nprompts/55-continuity-bible.md\nprompts/28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.md\nprompts/32-ui-action-policy-engine.md\nprompts/94-performance-pass.md\nprompts/29-frontend-websocket-client-skeleton.md\nprompts/58-usage-cost-and-latency-metrics.md\nprompts/68-autosave-drafts-and-partials.md\nprompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.md\nprompts/91-rate-limits-retries-and-fallbacks.md\nprompts/24-design-tokens-and-accessibility-foundation.md\nprompts/84-reader-ui-with-navigation.md\nprompts/59-planning-funnel-e2e-test.md\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:13:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/00-project-charter.md\"","aggregated_output":"# 00 — Project Charter and Success Criteria\n\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\n\n## Objective\n\nCreate the initial project framing for a full-stack app that helps a user build a bedtime story, turn it into audio, and resume past sessions.\n\n## Build\n- Write a top-level README that explains the product vision, the end-to-end story workflow, the required stages, and the major technical choices already fixed by the brief.\n- Document the non-negotiable requirements: React + Vite frontend, Python FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL for all structured data, a file-backed GCS emulator for blobs, Docker Compose for local orchestration, and `secrets.yaml` kept out of git.\n- Add a short architecture note describing why all Gemini calls must happen on the backend and why long-running composition and audio jobs need resumable server-side state.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- `README.md` with product overview and local development expectations\n- `docs/product-brief.md` or equivalent design note\n- `docs/architecture-overview.md` with the first cut of the system picture\n\n## Acceptance checks\n\n- A new engineer can read the repo root and understand what is being built.\n- The required story stages from the brief are explicitly listed in order.\n- The first screen requirement — a past-sessions screen for resume/edit/create — is called out early, not buried.\n\n## Notes\n\nDo not generate implementation code yet beyond lightweight documentation scaffolding.\n\n## Suggested commit label\n\n`feat(prompt-00): project charter`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\n\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\n\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\n\n## Product Summary\n\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\n\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\n   - Genre selection\n   - Tone selection\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\n   - Story pitches\n   - Character sheet\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\n   - Composition\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\n\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\n\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\n\n- Wonder is welcome.\n- Adventure is welcome.\n- Mystery is welcome.\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\n- Emotional repair matters.\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\n\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\n\n## Required Story Workflow\n\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\n\n1. **Genre Selection**\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\n2. **Tone Selection**\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\n3. **Story Setup**\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\n4. **Story Pitches**\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\n5. **Character Sheet**\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\n6. **Story Beats**\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\n8. **Composition**\n   - The system writes in segments.\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\n9. **Audio**\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\n10. **Finalize**\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\n\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\n\n### Frontend\n- React\n- Vite\n- TypeScript\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\n\n### Backend\n- Python\n- FastAPI\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\n\n### Data and Infrastructure\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\n\n### AI\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\n- Use separate adapters for:\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\n  - long-form story generation\n  - narration / TTS\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\n\n## Recommended Architecture\n\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\n\n### Suggested application shape\n- `frontend/` — React + Vite app\n- `backend/` — FastAPI app plus domain services\n- `infra/` — Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\n- `docs/` — architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\n\n### Suggested backend layers\n- `api/` or route handlers\n- `settings/`\n- `db/`\n- `models/`\n- `repositories/`\n- `services/`\n- `worker/`\n- `ai/`\n- `storage/`\n\n### Suggested durable concepts\n- Session\n- Workflow stage\n- Event log\n- Selected genre\n- Selected tone\n- Story brief\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\n- Beat sheet\n- Story setup preferences\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\n- Continuity bible\n- Composition job\n- Composition segment\n- Audio job\n- Asset record\n\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\n\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\n\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\n\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\n\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\n\n### Chat-to-UI\nA user message can propose actions like:\n- select a tone\n- regenerate pitches\n- choose a pitch\n- adjust character traits\n- soften the midpoint\n- shorten the story target\n- pause writing\n- change narration speed\n\n### UI-to-Chat\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\n- “Selected genre: Quest Fantasy”\n- “Updated target runtime: ~12 minutes”\n- “Accepted revised beat sheet”\n\n### Safety rule\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\n\n## Save The Cat Expectations\n\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\n\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\n\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\n\nUse the included research docs to seed:\n- a curated genre list\n- per-genre tone options\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\n\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\n\n## Audio Expectations\n\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\n\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\n- Support voice selection\n- Support speed controls\n- Support optional background music\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\n\n## Export Expectations\n\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\n- final story text in the app\n- final audio in the app\n- downloadable `.docx`\n- downloadable final audio file\n\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\n\nFavor:\n- typed interfaces\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\n- migrations\n- tests around durable state\n- visible logs\n- simple scripts for local dev\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:38:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_20","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' docs/product-brief.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Brief\n\n## Product Summary\n\nStoryteller is a session-based bedtime story creation studio. It helps a user move from a loose story idea to a finished written story and finished narration audio, with the ability to leave and come back later without losing progress.\n\nThe first meaningful screen is the sessions home. It must foreground past sessions, including both in-progress and completed stories, so resume and revisit are the default behaviors rather than an afterthought.\n\n## User Experience Principles\n\n- Bedtime-first: stories should support calm endings, emotional repair, and low-friction read-aloud use.\n- Guided, not opaque: users move through explicit workflow stages instead of one giant prompt box.\n- Bidirectional control: chat can suggest UI actions, and UI actions must be echoed back into chat.\n- Durable by default: progress survives refreshes, restarts, and long-running generation work.\n- Local-first development: the full stack should run locally through Docker Compose with persistent infrastructure.\n\n## End-to-End Workflow\n\nThe product must support this ordered flow:\n\n1. Genre selection from a curated genre list.\n2. Tone selection from options preconfigured for the chosen genre.\n3. Story setup through a free-form brief describing the desired story.\n4. Story pitches generated by Gemini, with selection, refinement, or regeneration.\n5. Character sheet generation, again with selection and refinement.\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet generation and editing.\n7. Story setup preferences for soft targets such as word count, runtime, and chapters.\n8. Composition in segments with visible progress, typewriter-like output, and interruptibility.\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation, including voice and speed controls plus optional background music.\n10. Finalize, where the user can read, listen, and download story and audio artifacts.\n\n## Core Product Behaviors\n\n### Sessions and Resume\n\n- Sessions are durable backend entities.\n- A user can reopen a session from any stage.\n- In-progress and completed sessions both appear on the home screen.\n\n### Workspace Layout\n\n- Desktop layout uses two panes.\n- Left pane is the chat workspace, about one-third width.\n- Right pane is the main workflow UI, about two-thirds width.\n\n### Chat and UI Bridge\n\n- User chat can propose structured actions such as selecting a tone or refining beats.\n- The model may propose actions, but a deterministic backend policy layer decides whether each action is valid for the session's current stage.\n- Direct UI actions must create compact chat-visible summaries for auditability and replay.\n\n### Composition Experience\n\n- Story writing happens in segments.\n- Progress is visible and should feel meaningfully live.\n- The system should periodically summarize what it is writing in chat.\n- Users can interrupt, redirect, and request rewrites.\n\n### Audio Experience\n\n- Narration is a first-class feature, not a demo extra.\n- Audio generation is segmented and resumable.\n- The app estimates likely runtime before generation starts.\n- Final audio should be playable in-app and downloadable.\n\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Constraints\n\n- Frontend: React, Vite, TypeScript\n- Backend: Python, FastAPI\n- Structured persistence: PostgreSQL\n- Object storage in local development: file-backed GCS emulator\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\n- Secrets: local `secrets.yaml`, excluded from git\n- AI model family: Gemini 3.1, with backend-owned adapters and centralized model configuration\n\n## Deliverable Expectations\n\nThe finished product should let a user:\n\n- create and revise a bedtime story plan\n- generate and edit a complete story\n- generate, play, and download narration audio\n- download the story as `.docx`\n- resume work from prior sessions without losing context\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:39:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/architecture-overview.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Architecture Overview\n\n## System Picture\n\nStoryteller is a full-stack application with a browser client, a backend-owned workflow engine, durable relational state, durable object storage, and resumable background jobs.\n\n```text\nReact + Vite client\n        |\n        v\nFastAPI API layer\n        |\n        +--> domain services and policy layer\n        |         |\n        |         +--> AI adapters for planning, composition, and narration\n        |         |\n        |         +--> repositories for sessions, workflow state, jobs, and event log\n        |\n        +--> background workers for composition and audio generation\n                  |\n                  +--> PostgreSQL for durable structured state\n                  +--> file-backed GCS emulator for artifacts and intermediate blobs\n```\n\n## Why Gemini Calls Must Stay on the Backend\n\nAll Gemini access belongs on the server side for four reasons:\n\n1. Secrets control: provider credentials live in local `secrets.yaml` and must never enter the browser.\n2. Policy enforcement: chat-to-UI actions and workflow transitions need deterministic server validation before the model can change session state.\n3. Durable orchestration: prompts, structured outputs, retries, and model selection need to be coordinated with persistent session data and job records.\n4. Provider flexibility: keeping Gemini behind backend adapters lets the app keep a stable internal interface while model IDs, structured output tactics, or narration providers evolve.\n\nThe frontend should only call the backend's API and subscribe to backend-owned progress events.\n\n## Why Composition and Audio Must Use Resumable Server-Side Jobs\n\nComposition and narration are long-running operations that cannot safely live inside request threads or browser memory. They need server-side jobs because:\n\n- a user must be able to refresh the page or return later without losing progress\n- partial story text and audio segments need durable checkpoints\n- progress updates need a single source of truth that both chat and UI can observe\n- retries, cancellation, and resume logic need explicit job state rather than transient in-memory state\n\nThe durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after interruption.\n\n## Intended Backend Shape\n\nThe target backend should separate concerns explicitly:\n\n- `api/`: HTTP routes and websocket endpoints\n- `settings/`: environment and secrets loading\n- `db/`: database engine, migrations, and persistence setup\n- `models/`: typed domain models and persistence schemas\n- `repositories/`: database access for sessions, events, jobs, and assets\n- `services/`: workflow logic and policy enforcement\n- `ai/`: Gemini planning, composition, and narration adapters\n- `worker/`: background job execution for writing and audio\n- `storage/`: object storage abstraction over the local GCS emulator\n\n## Durable Domain Concepts\n\nThe system should persist the following concepts as first-class records:\n\n- Session\n- Workflow stage\n- Event log\n- Selected genre\n- Selected tone\n- Story brief\n- Pitch batch and selected pitch\n- Character batch and selected character sheet\n- Beat sheet\n- Story setup preferences\n- Outline or chapter plan\n- Continuity bible\n- Composition job and segments\n- Audio job and segments\n- Asset metadata\n\n## Current Repository State\n\nThe repository currently contains an initial scaffold:\n\n- `frontend/`: Vite React app\n- `backend/`: FastAPI app\n- `docker-compose.yml`: local frontend, backend, and browser QA services\n- `tools/webapp-qa/`: containerized browser automation support\n\nPostgreSQL, the file-backed GCS emulator, background workers, and most domain layers are still planned work. This document records the required direction so later prompts can extend the scaffold without changing the architectural contract.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:40:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_21","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' README.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Storyteller\n\nStoryteller is a local-first, full-stack app for creating bedtime stories from first idea to finished text and finished narration audio. The product is session-based: the first meaningful screen is a past-sessions view where a user can resume work in progress, reopen a completed story, or start a new session.\n\nThis repository currently contains the first scaffold for that system. The project charter in this README and the supporting docs define the product and architecture that later prompts will implement.\n\n## Product Vision\n\nThe app should feel like a guided bedtime story studio, not a generic prompt box. A user should be able to:\n\n- See past sessions first and continue where they left off.\n- Collaborate through both chat and direct UI controls.\n- Move through a structured planning workflow before long-form writing starts.\n- Watch story composition and audio generation progress in real time.\n- Return later to read, listen, download, or revise completed work.\n\nThe tone of the product should stay calm, readable, and trustworthy. Wonder, mystery, and adventure are welcome, but bedtime suitability is a hard requirement: emotional stakes should resolve safely and the ending should feel restful.\n\n## Required Story Workflow\n\nThe application is being built around this ordered workflow:\n\n1. Genre selection\n2. Tone selection\n3. Story setup / free-form brief\n4. Story pitches\n5. Character sheet\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet\n7. Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\n8. Composition\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation\n10. Finalize / read / listen / download\n\nEach stage must be durable, resumable, and editable. Chat messages can propose actions in the workflow, and direct UI actions must also be reflected back into the session chat log as compact summaries.\n\n## Fixed Technical Decisions\n\nThese choices are not optional for this project:\n\n- Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript\n- Backend: Python + FastAPI\n- Structured data: PostgreSQL\n- Blob storage: file-backed GCS emulator in local development\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\n- Secrets handling: local `secrets.yaml`, never committed to git\n- AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services\n\nTwo architectural rules follow from those constraints:\n\n- The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.\n- Long-running story composition and narration generation must be durable background jobs with resumable server-side state.\n\nMore detail is in [docs/product-brief.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/product-brief.md) and [docs/architecture-overview.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/architecture-overview.md).\n\n## Local Development Expectations\n\nThe repo is intended to run locally with Docker Compose. At the current scaffold stage:\n\n- `frontend/` contains a Vite React app served on `http://localhost:8566`\n- `backend/` contains a FastAPI app served on `http://localhost:8565`\n- `tools/webapp-qa/` contains the browser automation container used for local UI verification\n\nStart the current stack with:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose up --build\n```\n\nThe current scaffold does not yet include PostgreSQL or the file-backed GCS emulator, but both are required parts of the target system and will be added through later prompt work. `secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials.\n\n## Repository Shape\n\n- `frontend/`: React + Vite client application\n- `backend/`: FastAPI application and future domain services\n- `docs/`: product and architecture notes\n- `prompts/`: sequential build prompts and task summaries\n- `tools/`: local QA and developer tooling\n\n## What Success Looks Like\n\nA new engineer should be able to open this repository and quickly understand:\n\n- the first screen is the past-sessions home for resume, edit, and create flows\n- the story workflow is staged and ordered\n- the final product writes stories and generates narration audio durably\n- the backend owns AI access, long-running jobs, and persistent state\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:41:{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_22","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"python - <<'PY'\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfiles = {\n    'README.md': Path('README.md').read_text(),\n    'docs/product-brief.md': Path('docs/product-brief.md').read_text(),\n    'docs/architecture-overview.md': Path('docs/architecture-overview.md').read_text(),\n}\nrequired_stages = [\n    'Genre selection',\n    'Tone selection',\n    'Story setup',\n    'Story pitches',\n    'Character sheet',\n    'Save-the-Cat beat sheet',\n    'Story setup preferences',\n    'Composition',\n    'Audio configuration and audio generation',\n    'Finalize / read / listen / download',\n]\nprint('files_present:', all(Path(p).exists() for p in files))\nprint('readme_mentions_past_sessions_early:', 'first meaningful screen is a past-sessions view' in files['README.md'])\nfor stage in required_stages:\n    print(stage, stage in files['README.md'])\nchecks = {\n    'react_vite_typescript': all(term in files['README.md'] for term in ['React + Vite + TypeScript']),\n    'fastapi_backend': 'Python + FastAPI' in files['README.md'],\n    'postgres': 'PostgreSQL' in files['README.md'],\n    'gcs_emulator': 'file-backed GCS emulator' in files['README.md'],\n    'docker_compose': 'Docker Compose' in files['README.md'],\n    'secrets_yaml': 'secrets.yaml' in files['README.md'],\n    'backend_gemini_note': 'Gemini' in files['docs/architecture-overview.md'] and 'backend' in files['docs/architecture-overview.md'],\n    'resumable_jobs_note': 'resumable server-side jobs' in files['docs/architecture-overview.md'],\n}\nfor name, ok in checks.items():\n    print(name, ok)\nPY\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}

The README now calls out the past-sessions-first home screen early, lists the full staged workflow in order, and documents the fixed stack and local development expectations. The architecture note records the backend-only Gemini boundary and the requirement for resumable server-side composition/audio jobs.\n\nVerification was docs-focused: I manually reviewed all three files, ran a Python acceptance check covering file presence, first-screen wording, ordered workflow, fixed stack documentation, backend Gemini rationale, resumable jobs rationale, and sessions-home coverage in the product brief, and ran `git diff --check`, which passed. No tests, builds, browser checks, or screenshots were applicable because this task was documentation-only.\n\nI created the requested checkpoint commit `feat(prompt-00): project charter`, and the required reviewer summary file was written last at [prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md)."}}

    prompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.summary.md:52:These are intentionally behavior-shaped rather than implementation-shaped. Each test crosses real persistence boundaries by reopening SQLAlchemy sessions after commits and, for storage, by reading back bytes and metadata from the fake GCS server.
    prompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.summary.md:148:- event log persistence
    prompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.summary.md:163:- the 5 skipped tests are the new integration tests, intentionally skipped in the default fast path
    prompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.summary.md:228:I intentionally did not build bucket-deletion cleanup into the harness. The suite isolates storage writes through unique object paths and isolates relational state with disposable databases and table truncation. That is simpler and reliable with the current fake-GCS setup, which is enough for this prompt’s durability focus.

    prompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md:1969:    Added the project charter docs at [README.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/README.md), [docs/product-brief.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/product-brief.md), and [docs/architecture-overview.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/architecture-overview.md). The README now calls out the past-sessions-first home screen early, lists the full staged workflow in order, and documents the fixed stack and local development expectations. The architecture note records the backend-only Gemini boundary and the requirement for resumable server-side composition/audio jobs.
    prompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md:1971:    Verification was docs-focused: I manually reviewed all three files, ran a Python acceptance check covering file presence, first-screen wording, ordered workflow, fixed stack documentation, backend Gemini rationale, resumable jobs rationale, and sessions-home coverage in the product brief, and ran `git diff --check`, which passed. No tests, builds, browser checks, or screenshots were applicable because this task was documentation-only.

    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9274:    - Collaborate through both chat and direct UI controls.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9296:    Each stage must be durable, resumable, and editable. Chat messages can propose actions in the workflow, and direct UI actions must also be reflected back into the session chat log as compact summaries.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9308:    - AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9312:    - The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9496:    - Added placeholder READMEs in the major folders so a new engineer can infer intent without reading the prompt pack first:
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9788:      - The visual language is intentionally calmer and more product-specific than the generic template.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9844:    The current router is intentionally small. To add another route later, extend `src/app/router.tsx`:
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:9996:    - I intentionally ran this before starting the backend to confirm the frontend still boots in isolation.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10202:            |         +--> repositories for sessions, workflow state, jobs, and event log
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10210:    ## Why Gemini Calls Must Stay on the Backend
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10212:    All Gemini access belongs on the server side for four reasons:
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10215:    2. Policy enforcement: chat-to-UI actions and workflow transitions need deterministic server validation before the model can change session state.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10217:    4. Provider flexibility: keeping Gemini behind backend adapters lets the app keep a stable internal interface while model IDs, structured output tactics, or narration providers evolve.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10227:    - progress updates need a single source of truth that both chat and UI can observe
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10230:    The durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after interruption.
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:10242:    - `ai/`: Gemini planning, composition, and narration adapters
    prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.yolopilot.md:15286:    Verification passed with backend pytest, `compileall`, live local startup plus `curl` checks, Docker Compose startup with the new `/health` healthcheck, and the existing browser smoke spec. The required reviewer summary was written last to [03-fastapi-scaffold.summary.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.summary.md). Current intentional limit: database wiring is still a scaffold and reports `not-configured` until later prompts.

_enable_sqlite_foreign_keys(engine) -> None:\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        connection.exec_driver_sql(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON\")\n\n\ndef _as_set(values: Iterable[str]) -> set[str]:\n    return set(values)\n\n\ndef test_story_schema_can_store_in_progress_and_completed_sessions() -> None:\n    engine = make_engine(\"sqlite+pysqlite:///:memory:\")\n    _enable_sqlite_foreign_keys(engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)\n    db_session = sessionmaker(bind=engine, autoflush=False, expire_on_commit=False)()\n\n    try:\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        genre = Genre(\n            slug=\"quest-fantasy\",\n            label=\"Quest Fantasy\",\n            description=\"A gentle adventure with bedtime-safe stakes.\",\n        )\n        tone = ToneProfile(\n            genre=genre,\n            slug=\"hushed-wonder\",\n            label=\"Hushed Wonder\",\n            description=\"Calm and luminous.\",\n            default_planning_hints={\"pacing\": \"unhurried\"},\n        )\n\n        draft_session = StorySession(\n            working_title=\"Moonlit Boat Ride\",\n            current_stage=WorkflowStage.COMPOSITION,\n            resume_stage=WorkflowStage.COMPOSITION,\n            furthest_completed_stage=WorkflowStage.STORY_SETUP,\n            overall_status=WorkflowStageState.IN_PROGRESS,\n            selected_genre=genre,\n            selected_tone_profile=tone,\n        )\n        completion_event = EventLogEntry(\n            session=draft_session,\n            sequence_number=1,\n            actor_type=EventActorType.USER,\n            actor_id=\"local-user\",\n            event_type=\"accepted_story_setup\",\n            stage=WorkflowStage.STORY_SETUP,\n            summary=\"Accepted the current story setup targets.\",\n        )\n        draft_session.workflow_stage_states.extend(\n            [\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.GENRE,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                    last_event=completion_event,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.TONE,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.BRIEF,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.PITCHES,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.CHARACTERS,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.BEATS,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.STORY_SETUP,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.COMPLETED,\n                    completed_at=now,\n                ),\n                WorkflowStageSnapshot(\n                    stage=WorkflowStage.COMPOSITION,\n                    status=WorkflowStageState.IN_PROGRESS,\n                    started_at=now,\n                ),\n            ],\n        )\n        brief = StoryBrief(\n            session=draft_session,\n            revision_number=1,\n            raw_brief=\"A sleepy fox rows across a moonlit lake.\",\n            normalized_summary=(\n                \"A bedtime-safe quest about crossing the lake to find a glowing reed.\"\n            ),\n            is_active=True,\n            accepted_at=now,\n        )\n        pitch = Pitch(\n            session=draft_session,\n            story_brief=brief,\n            generation_key=\"pitch-batch-1\",\n            pitch_index=0,\n            title=\"The Reed of Quiet Light\",\n            logline=\"A young fox follows ripples toward a gentle night mystery.\",\n            is_selected=True,\n            accepted_at=now,\n        )\n        character_sheet = CharacterSheet(\n            session=draft_session,\n            pitch=pitch,\n            revision_number=1,\n            protagonist_name=\"Pip\",\n            summary=\"Pip is cautious, curious, and soothed by steady rhythms.\",\n            is_selected=True,\n            accepted_at=now,\n        )\n        beat_sheet = BeatSheet(\n            session=draft_session,\n            character_sheet=character_sheet,\n            revision_number=1,\n            summary=\"A soft Save-the-Cat arc with a reassuring return home.\",\n            beats={\"opening_image\": \"Moonlight on still water\"},\n            is_selected=True,\n            accepted_at=now,\n        )\n        story_setup = StorySetup(\n            session=draft_session,\n            beat_sheet=beat_sheet,\n            revision_number=1,\n            target_word_count=1800,\n            target_runtime_minutes=12,\n            chapter_count=3,\n            chapter_style=\"three gentle chapters\",\n            is_selected=True,\n            accepted_at=now,\n        )\n        composition_job = CompositionJob(\n            session=draft_session,\n            beat_sheet=beat_sheet,\n            story_setup=story_setup,\n            job_kind=CompositionJobKind.DRAFT,\n            status=JobStatus.IN_PROGRESS,\n            progress_percent=42.5,\n            current_segment_index=2,\n        )\n        composition_segment = CompositionSegment(\n            session=draft_session,\n            composition_job=composition_job,\n            segment_index=2,\n            revision_number=1,\n            status=JobStatus.IN_PROGRESS,\n            planned_summary=\"Pip reaches the reeds and hears the lake settle.\",\n        )\n\n        completed_session = StorySession(\n            working_title=\"The Lantern Nest\",\n            current_stage=WorkflowStage.FINALIZE,\n            resume_stage=WorkflowStage.FINALIZE,\n            furthest_completed_stage=WorkflowStage.FINALIZE,\n AudioJob(\n            session=completed_session,\n            source_composition_job=composition_job,\n            status=JobStatus.COMPLETED,\n            voice_key=\"gemini-soft-1\",\n session=completed_session,\n            audio_job=audio_job,\n            asset_kind=AssetKind.FINAL_AUDIO,\n            status=AssetStatus.READY,\n )\n\n        db_session.add_all(\n            [\n                genre,\n                tone,\n                draft_session,\n                completion_event,\n                brief,\n completed_session,\n                audio_job,\n                final_audio,\n            ]\n        )\n        db_session.commit()\n        db_session.expire_all()\n\n        session_rows = (\n     prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:56:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "# 15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail\n\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\n\n## Objective\n\nCreate an append-only event history that can explain how a session changed over time and later power resume, replay, and debugging.\n\n## Build\n- Define an event log table or model that captures actor, event type, payload, timestamps, and optional stage context.\n- Record meaningful events for session creation, selections, AI outputs, user edits, chat messages, UI actions, composition progress, and audio progress.\n- Add helper utilities for appending events in a consistent way.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- Event log model and access layer\n- Common event helpers\n- Documentation for the event taxonomy\n\n## Acceptance checks\n\n- A session\u2019s history can be reconstructed without relying on browser local storage.\n- Events distinguish between user actions, agent actions, and system/worker actions.\n- Event payloads are typed or at least versioned enough to age well.\n\n## Notes\n\nThink of this as product telemetry plus durable replay support.\n\n## Suggested commit label\n\n`feat(prompt-15): event log and audit trail`\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_26", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 56, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:36:32-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:57:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' docs/domain-model.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "# Domain Model and Session State Machine\n\nThis document defines the durable business objects and workflow rules for a single story-creation session. The goal is to give the backend, frontend, migrations, and later AI orchestration code one shared contract for how a session progresses, pauses, rewinds, and resumes.\n\n## Core Modeling Rules\n\n- The `story_session` is the durable unit of work.\n- Workflow truth lives on the backend, not in browser-only UI state.\n- Every workflow stage is tracked explicitly, even when later stages are generated from earlier ones.\n- Backward edits are allowed on purpose and must mark downstream outputs stale instead of silently pretending they are still current.\n- Composition and audio are long-running workflows backed by durable records, not one-shot request responses.\n- Event history is append-only and explains how the current snapshot was reached.\n\n## Session Snapshot Contract\n\nThe session snapshot returned to the UI should eventually include these fields, even if some land in later prompts:\n\n| Field | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `id` | Stable UUID for the story session. |\n| `working_title` | Best current human-readable label for lists and search. |\n| `current_stage` | Stage the user is actively viewing or editing right now. |\n| `resume_stage` | Earliest stage that still needs work when a session is reopened. |\n| `furthest_completed_stage` | Highest ordered stage that is still valid and completed. |\n| `overall_status` | Session-level rollup such as `draft`, `in_progress`, `completed`, or `needs_regeneration`. |\n| `selected_genre_id` | Accepted genre catalog row for the session. |\n| `selected_tone_profile_id` | Accepted tone profile row for the session. |\n| `selected_pitch_id` | Accepted pitch record. |\n| `selected_character_sheet_id` | Accepted character-sheet record. |\n| `accepted_beat_sheet_id` | Accepted beat-sheet record. |\n| `story_setup_id` | Accepted story-setup preferences record. |\n| `active_composition_job_id` | Current composition job, if writing or rewrite work is running. |\n| `active_audio_job_id` | Current audio job, if narration work is running. |\n| `latest_story_asset_id` | Most recent readable story artifact or aggregate text record. |\n| `latest_audio_asset_id` | Most recent playable final audio artifact. |\n| `created_at`, `updated_at`, `completed_at` | Audit and list-view timestamps. |\n\n`resume_stage` is the key anti-guessing field. The backend computes it from durable stage states so the frontend does not need to infer where to reopen a session by looking at which panels happen to have data.\n\nPrompt 11 keeps the relational core intentionally one-directional where practical: child records\nsuch as pitches, character sheets, beat sheets, and setup revisions point back to the owning\nsession, and the accepted row is tracked on the child record itself. The API snapshot can still\nsurface selected child IDs without forcing the first migration into a web of circular foreign keys.\n\n## Major Entities\n\n| Entity | Durable role | Key fields | Notes |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| `story_session` | Root aggregate for one bedtime-story project. | IDs, title, stage pointers, overall status, timestamps. | Owns the current accepted choices and job pointers. |\n| `workflow_stage_state` | Per-stage state for the session. | `session_id`, `stage`, `status`, `updated_at`, `last_event_id`. | Stored explicitly so resume does not depend on sparse child tables. |\n| `genre` | Curated genre catalog entry. | slug, label, description, bedtime-safety notes, arc notes. | Backend-owned reference data. |\n| `tone_profile` | Curated tone option linked to a genre. | `genre_id`, slug, label, descriptors, bedtime notes, default planning hints. | Tone choices are filtered by genre. |\n| `story_brief` | User-authored idea plus any normalized planning summary. | raw brief text, normalized summary, revision number. | Keeps user input separate from later generated planning outputs. |\n| `pitch` | One candidate story premise. | batch or generation group, summary, hook, bedtime notes, selection flag. | `selected_pitch_id` on the session represents the accepted pitch; selection does not require a separate table. |\n| `character_sheet` | Accepted cast and character traits for the chosen direction. | protagonist/supporting cast data, revision, generation metadata. | Multiple revisions may exist; the session points at the accepted one. |\n| `beat_sheet` | Structured Save-the-Cat plan. | ordered beats, bedtime-adaptation notes, revision. | Must be editable and versioned. |\n| `story_setup` | Soft planning targets. | target words, target runtime, chapter count, chapter style, notes. | These are guides, not hard constraints. |\n| `composition_job` | Parent job record for writing or rewriting text. | status, progress, attempt count, stop reason, current segment pointer. | Not listed in the prompt title, but needed to make `composition_segment` durable and resumable. |\n| `composition_segment` | One planned or written segment of the story. | segment index, planned summary, text, revision number, superseded segment, status. | Supports interruption, partial persistence, and rewrites of earlier sections. |\n| `audio_job` | Parent job record for narration generation. | voice, speed, music setting, progress, status, compiled asset pointer. | Audio should be resumable and segment-aware. |\n| `export_asset` | Durable artifact metadata for text and audio outputs. | asset kind, storage key, MIME type, byte size, checksum, readiness status. | Covers `.docx`, final audio, and intermediate durable artifacts when needed. |\n| `event_log_entry` | Append-only session history. | actor, event type, stage context, payload, created_at, version. | Supports replay, resume hydration, debugging, and audit. |\n\n## Canonical Workflow Stages\n\nThese lowercase identifiers are the wire-format contract. API payloads should use the IDs, not numeric indexes or UI labels.\n\n| Order | Stage ID | UI label | Stage completes when |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| 1 | `genre` | Genre | The user accepts a genre for the session. |\n| 2 | `tone` | Tone | The user accepts a tone profile for the selected genre. |\n| 3 | `brief` | Story brief | The session has an accepted free-form brief and any required normalized summary. |\n| 4 | `pitches` | Pitches | One pitch is accepted or the user intentionally keeps a current pitch set for further refinement. |\n| 5 | `characters` | Characters | One character sheet is accepted. |\n| 6 | `beats` | Beat sheet | One beat sheet is accepted. |\n| 7 | `story_setup` | Story setup | The user accepts soft targets such as runtime and chapter plan. |\n| 8 | `composition` | Composition | The current story text is complete enough to hand off to narration. |\n| 9 | `audio` | Audio | Final narration assets are generated for the current story text and settings. |\n| 10 | `finalize` | Finalize | Read/listen/download assets are ready for the current accepted story state. |\n\nCurrent code mirrors this contract in:\n\n- `backend/app/models/workflow.py`\n- `frontend/src/features/session/workflowStages.ts`\n\nThe backend remains the authority for validating transitions. The frontend should use the same literal IDs for rendering, navigation, and optimistic display only.\n\n## Stage State Semantics\n\nThe same four-state lifecycle should be used for stage rows and for generated artifacts that can become stale.\n\n| State | Meaning | Example |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| `draft` | The stage has no accepted durable output yet. Partial input may exist, but the stage is not considered complete. | The user typed part of a brief but has not accepted it yet. |\n| `in_progress` | Durable work is actively being produced or updated. | Pitch generation is running, composition is streaming segments, or audio rendering is underway. |\n| `completed` | The stage has an accepted output that is still valid relative to all upstream dependencies. | A beat sheet was accepted and no upstream planning change has made it stale. |\n| `needs_regeneration` | The stage was completed before, but an upstream change made its accepted output stale. The old output may remain visible for comparison, but it is not the current source of truth. | The user edits characters after a beat sheet exists, so beats, composition, audio, and finalize become stale. |\n\n### Session-Level Rollup\n\nThe session-level `overall_status` should be derived, not manually entered:\n\n- `draft`: no stage has reached `completed` yet.\n- `in_progress`: at least one stage is `in_progress`, or some stages are completed but the session has not reached a valid finalization state.\n- `completed`: `finalize` is `completed` and no stage is `needs_regeneration`.\n- `needs_regeneration`: one or more stages are `needs_regeneration`, even if the session also has readable prior outputs.\n\n## Allowed Forward Progression\n\nThe default happy-path order is linear:\n\n`genre -> tone -> brief -> pitches -> characters -> beats -> story_setup -> composition -> audio -> finalize`\n\nForward movement rule:\n\n- The user may enter the next stage once the current stage is `completed`.\n\nResume rule:\n\n- On reopen, the backend should send the earliest stage whose state is not `completed`.\n- If all stages are `completed`, `resume_stage` is `finalize`.\n\nThis is the behavior implemented by the `resolve_resume_stage` helper in both the backend and frontend contract files.\n\n## Safe Backward Edits and Regeneration Rules\n\nBackward navigation is allowed to any already-reached earlier stage. The important rule is what happens after the user accepts a change there.\n\n| Edited stage | Stages marked `needs_regeneration` after acceptance |\n| --- | --- |\n| `genre` | `tone`, `brief`, `pitches`, `characters`, `beats`, `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `tone` | `brief`, `pitches`, `characters`, `beats`, `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `brief` | `pitches`, `characters`, `beats`, `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `pitches` | `characters`, `beats`, `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `characters` | `beats`, `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `beats` | `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `story_setup` | `composition`, `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `composition` | `audio`, `finalize` |\n| `audio` | `finalize` |\n| `finalize` | none |\n\nWhy `story_setup` is not invalidated by earlier planning edits:\n\n- Word count, runtime, and chapter preferences are user intent, not generated story content.\n- They still influence later composition, but a new pitch or character sheet does not automatically erase the user's preferred target length.\n\n## Composition Interruption and Rewrite Rules\n\nComposition is intentionally more flexible than the earlier planning stages.\n\n- A composition job may move between `in_progress`, paused, resumed, or cancelled states without leaving the `composition` stage.\n- Partial text is persisted segment by segment so a refresh or crash does not erase progress.\n- A rewrite request against an earlier segment creates a new `composition_job` or new segment revision rather than mutating history in place.\n- Superseded segments remain durable for comparison and audit.\n- Any accepted composition rewrite marks `audio` and `finalize` as `needs_regeneration`.\n- If the user decides the plan itself is wrong, they may navigate back to `beats` or `story_setup`, accept changes there, and let the backend mark `composition` and later stages stale.\n\n## Re-entry From Past Sessions\n\nThe system should support these reopen flows without guessing from UI state:\n\n1. Planning session reopened midstream:\n   - Example: `genre`, `tone`, and `brief` are `completed`; `pitches` is `in_progress`.\n   - Result: open the session on `resume_stage = pitches`.\n2. Finished draft reopened for upstream change:\n   - Example: `composition`, `audio`, and `finalize` were `completed`, then the user edits the beat sheet.\n   - Result: `composition`, `audio`, and `finalize` become `needs_regeneration`, and `resume_stage = composition`.\n3. Fully completed story reopened for reading only:\n   - Example: every stage is `completed`.\n   - Result: open on `resume_stage = finalize`, but still allow explicit navigation back to earlier stages for edits.\n\n## Shared Enum and Constants Plan\n\nThe repo does not yet have a generated cross-language schema package, so prompt 10 uses a boring mirrored-contract approach:\n\n- Backend authority: `backend/app/models/workflow.py`\n- Frontend mirror for rendering and client-side navigation: `frontend/src/features/session/workflowStages.ts`\n- Tests on both sides assert the same stage order, lifecycle states, and regeneration map.\n\nRules for later prompts:\n\n- Keep the literal stage IDs stable once APIs start returning them.\n- Keep the literal stage-state values stable once they enter database rows or websocket payloads.\n- If a later prompt introduces JSON Schema or code generation, generate from these same identifiers instead of renaming them.\n- The backend service layer, not the frontend, decides whether a requested stage transition is valid for the current session snapshot.\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' docs/domain-model.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_27", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 57, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:36:32-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:58:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Started", "message": "in progress: /bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n \"event log|event_log|audit|replay|telemetry\" docs backend/app backend/tests prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md'", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n \"event log|event_log|audit|replay|telemetry\" docs backend/app backend/tests prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md'", "exit_code": null, "id": "item_28", "status": "in_progress", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.started"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 58, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:36:32-07:00"}

log\ndocs/architecture-overview.md:51:The durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after Postgres\ndocs/adr/0001-core-runtime-architecture.md:15:- chat and direct UI actions both need an audit trail that can be replayed during resume\ndocs/adr/0001-core-runtime-architecture.md:49:Session resume should come from durable state, not browser cache. The event history is part of the product, not just an internal log, because it supports replay, auditability, and UI-to-chat echoes.\ndocs/adr/0001-core-runtime-architecture.md:96:- Resume and replay have a clear home in durable data.\ndocs/domain-model.md:62:| `event_log_entry` | Append-only session history. | actor, event type, stage context, payload, created_at, version. | Supports replay, resume hydration, debugging, and audit. |\ndocs/domain-model.md:154:- Superseded segments remain durable for comparison and audit.\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n \"event log|event_log|audit|replay|telemetry\" docs backend/app backend/tests prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md'", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_28", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 59, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:36:32-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:75:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Started", "message": "in progress: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\"", "exit_code": null, "id": "item_39", "status": "in_progress", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.started"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 75, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:37:39-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:76:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/62-composition-streaming-events.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "# 62 \u2014 Composition Streaming Events\n\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\n\n## Objective\n\nStream composition progress and text chunks to the frontend in a way that supports a live typewriter-style experience.\n\n## Build\n- Emit real-time events for segment start, chunk streamed, segment summary, overall progress, pause, rewrite, and completion.\n- Choose chunk sizes and timing that make the text feel lively and faster than typical reading pace without overwhelming the UI.\n- Persist enough progress metadata that the UI can recover after reconnect.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- Real-time composition event emission\n- Chunking strategy\n- Reconnect/recovery behavior for streaming state\n\n## Acceptance checks\n\n- The frontend can display a live writing experience rather than only final text dumps.\n- Chunk timing feels intentional and readable.\n- A reconnecting client can recover the latest accepted state.\n\n## Notes\n\nKeep event names aligned with the real-time schema from earlier prompts.\n\n## Suggested commit label\n\n`feat(prompt-62): composition streaming events`\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/62-composition-streaming-events.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_36", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 76, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:37:39-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:77:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "# 34 \u2014 Send UI Events Back Into Agent Context\n\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\n\n## Objective\n\nEnsure the agent can see important UI-side changes so follow-up chat turns are grounded in the current session rather than stale assumptions.\n\n## Build\n- Whenever the user changes a structured field in the UI, record a normalized event that can be fed into later model prompts or summaries.\n- Update the session snapshot and any rolling context summary used by the backend agent services.\n- Make sure UI-originated edits can trigger downstream regeneration flags when appropriate.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- Normalized UI event pipeline\n- Session snapshot update logic\n- Context summary update path\n\n## Acceptance checks\n\n- The agent can respond accurately after a user changes a field directly in the UI.\n- UI-originated updates are durable and replayable.\n- The context mechanism avoids re-reading the entire event history on every model call if practical.\n\n## Notes\n\nDo not rely on the browser alone as the source of truth.\n\n## Suggested commit label\n\n`feat(prompt-34): ui events to agent context`\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_37", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 77, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:37:39-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:79:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "# 33 \u2014 Reflect UI Actions Back Into the Chat Log\n\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\n\n## Objective\n\nMake UI changes and chat-driven actions visible inside the conversation so the user sees one coherent history of what happened.\n\n## Build\n- When a user clicks or changes something in the main pane, append a compact action message or summary into the chat history.\n- When a chat message produces an applied action, add an action echo that explains what changed in the UI.\n- Render these action echoes differently from normal assistant text so the history stays readable.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n- Chat action-echo rendering\n- Backend or frontend event wiring for echoes\n- Design rules for compact action summaries\n\n## Acceptance checks\n\n- The chat log and UI do not drift apart conceptually.\n- A resumed session can show how a key choice was made even if it was made in the UI rather than typed.\n- Action messages are concise and informative.\n\n## Notes\n\nThis feature is about trust and coherence.\n\n## Suggested commit label\n\n`feat(prompt-33): action echoes in chat`\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_39", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 79, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:37:39-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:83:{"color": "bright_black", "emoji": "\u2753", "event_type": "Unkown Event", "message": "{ \"item\": { \"id\": \"item_42\", \"items\": [ { \"completed\": false, \"text\": \"Add event taxonomy models plus repository/service helpers for append-only session events and history reads\" }, { \"completed\": false, \"text\": \"Wire event logging into...", "raw_event": {"item": {"id": "item_42", "items": [{"completed": false, "text": "Add event taxonomy models plus repository/service helpers for append-only session events and history reads"}, {"completed": false, "text": "Wire event logging into current session mutations and expose backend session history loading"}, {"completed": false, "text": "Expand tests and docs for event taxonomy, replay assumptions, and helper usage"}, {"completed": false, "text": "Run targeted verification, commit checkpoints, and write the required task summary file"}], "type": "todo_list"}, "type": "item.started"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 83, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:38:48-07:00"}
    prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.yolopilot.jsonlines:85:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/README.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "# Docs\n\nThis directory holds product notes, architecture notes, ADRs, diagrams, and other reviewer-oriented documentation.\n\nCurrent entrypoints:\n\n- `product-brief.md`: product expectations and workflow\n- `architecture-overview.md`: target system shape and durable boundaries\n- `domain-model.md`: durable session entities, workflow-stage contract, and stage-state rules\n- `genre-tone-catalog.md`: curated catalog provenance, editing rules, and seed command\n- `storage-buckets-and-prefixes.md`: bucket roles, stable object-key conventions, and storage usage\n- `system-diagram.md`: one-page runtime diagram for browser, API, worker, storage, and Gemini flows\n- `contributing.md`: shared quality commands and code-style conventions\n- `adr/`: architecture decision records, starting with the core runtime architecture ADR\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/README.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_43", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 85, "prompt_index": 16, "prompt_name": "15 \u2014 Event Log and Audit Trail", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:39:19-07:00"}

The backend now has a typed event taxonomy in [events.py](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/backend/app/models/events.py), an append/read repository in [events.py](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/backend/app/repositories/events.py), a higher-level logging service in [event_log.py](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/backend/app/services/event_log.py), and `SessionService` wiring in [sessions.py](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/backend/app/services/sessions.py) so session creation and stage transitions emit durable, versioned events and update `last_event_id` pointers on affected stage snapshots. I also documented the taxonomy in [event-taxonomy.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/event-taxonomy.md) and wrote the required reviewer summary in [15-event-log-and-audit-trail.summary.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/prompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.summary.md).\n\nVerification: `pytest backend/tests/test_event_log_service.py backend/tests/test_session_service.py` passed (`10 passed`), `pytest backend/tests` passed (`36 passed`), and `ruff check backend/app backend/tests` passed. No browser checks or screenshots were run because this prompt only changed backend/domain code.     prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:13:  - Documented the fixed technical choices from the brief: React + Vite + TypeScript, Python + FastAPI, PostgreSQL, a file-backed GCS emulator, Docker Compose, backend-owned Gemini access, and `secrets.yaml` excluded from git.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:18:  - Captured the bedtime-story positioning, the sessions-first UX requirement, the staged workflow, the two-pane workspace, and the chat/UI bridge rules.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:22:  - Explained why Gemini calls must stay on the backend.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:38:  - The frontend must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:39:  - Gemini usage should be isolated behind backend adapters for planning, long-form generation, and narration.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:43:  - PostgreSQL-backed job records, segment records, and event logs are part of the intended design.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:48:  - `docs/` now holds product and architecture intent.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:59:  - Example: if you are building pitch generation or beat-sheet editing, the product brief defines the required stage order and the expectation that chat and UI actions mirror each other.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:63:  - Example: if you later add Gemini integration, this doc already sets the boundary that secrets stay in `secrets.yaml` and model access stays behind backend services.
    prompts/00-project-charter.summary.md:118:- The only Gemini-related changes were architectural documentation notes.
