-messages-during-composition.md\\\\nprompts/41-tone-selection-api-and-ui.md\\\\nprompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines\\\\nprompts/23-frontend-state-foundation.md\\\\nprompts/97-security-review.md\\\\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.md\\\\nprompts/81-docx-export-pipeline.md\\\\nprompts/87-developer-debug-inspector.md\\\\nprompts/25-chat-window-foundation.md\\\\nprompts/01-monorepo-skeleton.codex.jsonlines\\\\nprompts/60-composition-job-orchestration.md\\\\nprompts/96-compose-dev-and-prod-shapes.md\\\\nprompts/49-beat-sheet-refinement-ui-and-chat.md\\\\nprompts/93-accessibility-pass-and-keyboard-support.md\\\\nprompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\\\\nprompts/47-character-refinement-via-chat.md\\\\nprompts/16-asset-metadata-and-file-records.md\\\\nprompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.yolopilot.md\\\\nprompts/76-audio-preview-and-segment-status-ui.md\\\\nprompts/53-outline-drill-down.md\\\\nprompts/66-rewrite-prior-segments.md\\\\nprompts/18-postgres-job-runner-skeleton.md\\\\nprompts/43-brief-normalization-service.md\\\\nprompts/base_prompt.md\\\\nprompts/50-story-length-duration-and-chapter-setup.md\\\\nprompts/86-completed-session-summary-and-search.md\\\\nprompts/82-html-markdown-and-reader-formatting.md\\\\nprompts/77-final-audio-assembly.md\\\\nprompts/79-audio-pipeline-tests.md\\\\nprompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\\\\nprompts/89-full-journey-e2e-test.md\\\\nprompts/57-composition-prompt-assembly.md\\\\nprompts/39-bridge-and-replay-tests.md\\\\nprompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.md\\\\nprompts/31-intent-parser-service.md\\\\nprompts/75-audio-job-orchestration.md\\\\nprompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.yolopilot.jsonlines\\\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md\\\\nprompts/85-audio-player-with-text-sync-hooks.md\\\\nprompts/00-project-charter.summary.md\\\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.md\\\\nprompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\\\\nprompts/08-repo-quality-tooling.md\\\\nprompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.md\\\\nprompts/73-gemini-tts-integration.md\\\\nprompts/71-audio-length-estimation.md\\\\nprompts/22-workspace-two-pane-layout.md\\\\nprompts/38-session-replay-and-resume-hydration.md\\\\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.md\\\\nprompts/40-genre-selection-api-and-ui.md\\\\nprompts/70-audio-settings-model-and-ui.md\\\\nprompts/69-composition-loop-e2e-test.md\\\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines\\\\nprompts/27-reusable-form-and-card-components.md\\\\nprompts/98-demo-data-and-onboarding.md\\\\nprompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.md\\\\nprompts/51-correlation-heuristics.md\\\\nprompts/35-chat-commands-and-quick-actions.md\\\\nprompts/55-continuity-bible.md\\\\nprompts/28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.md\\\\nprompts/32-ui-action-policy-engine.md\\\\nprompts/94-performance-pass.md\\\\nprompts/29-frontend-websocket-client-skeleton.md\\\\nprompts/58-usage-cost-and-latency-metrics.md\\\\nprompts/68-autosave-drafts-and-partials.md\\\\nprompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.md\\\\nprompts/91-rate-limits-retries-and-fallbacks.md\\\\nprompts/24-design-tokens-and-accessibility-foundation.md\\\\nprompts/84-reader-ui-with-navigation.md\\\\nprompts/59-planning-funnel-e2e-test.md\\\\nscripts/README.md\\\\nREADME.md\\\\ndocs/README.md\\\\ndocs/adr/README.md\\\\nbackend/README.md\\\\nbackend/requirements.txt\\\\nbackend/app/worker/README.md\\\\nbackend/migrations/README.md\\\\nfrontend/package.json\\\\nfrontend/README.md\\\\ntools/README.md\\\\ntools/webapp-qa/package.json\\\\ninfra/persistence/README.md\\\\ninfra/README.md\\\\ninfra/compose/docker-compose.yml\\\\ninfra/compose/README.md\\\\ntest-assets/README.md\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:839:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:22:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_10\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\\\n\\\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\\\n\\\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\\\n\\\\n## Product Summary\\\\n\\\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\\\n\\\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\\\n   - Genre selection\\\\n   - Tone selection\\\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\\\n   - Story pitches\\\\n   - Character sheet\\\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\\\n   - Composition\\\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\\\n\\\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\\\n\\\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\\\n\\\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\\\n\\\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\\\n\\\\n## Required Story Workflow\\\\n\\\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\\\n\\\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\\\n3. **Story Setup**\\\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\\\n6. **Story Beats**\\\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\\\n8. **Composition**\\\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\\\n9. **Audio**\\\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\\\n10. **Finalize**\\\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\\\n\\\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\\\n\\\\n### Frontend\\\\n- React\\\\n- Vite\\\\n- TypeScript\\\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\\\n\\\\n### Backend\\\\n- Python\\\\n- FastAPI\\\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\\\n\\\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\\\n\\\\n### AI\\\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\\\n  - long-form story generation\\\\n  - narration / TTS\\\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\\\n\\\\n## Recommended Architecture\\\\n\\\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\\\n\\\\n### Suggested application shape\\\\n- `frontend/` — React + Vite app\\\\n- `backend/` — FastAPI app plus domain services\\\\n- `infra/` — Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\\\n- `docs/` — architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\\\n\\\\n### Suggested backend layers\\\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\\\n- `settings/`\\\\n- `db/`\\\\n- `models/`\\\\n- `repositories/`\\\\n- `services/`\\\\n- `worker/`\\\\n- `ai/`\\\\n- `storage/`\\\\n\\\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\\\n- Session\\\\n- Workflow stage\\\\n- Event log\\\\n- Selected genre\\\\n- Selected tone\\\\n- Story brief\\\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\\\n- Beat sheet\\\\n- Story setup preferences\\\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\\\n- Continuity bible\\\\n- Composition job\\\\n- Composition segment\\\\n- Audio job\\\\n- Asset record\\\\n\\\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\\\n\\\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\\\n\\\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\\\n\\\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\\\n\\\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\\\n\\\\n### Chat-to-UI\\\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\\\n- select a tone\\\\n- regenerate pitches\\\\n- choose a pitch\\\\n- adjust character traits\\\\n- soften the midpoint\\\\n- shorten the story target\\\\n- pause writing\\\\n- change narration speed\\\\n\\\\n### UI-to-Chat\\\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\\\n- “Selected genre: Quest Fantasy”\\\\n- “Updated target runtime: ~12 minutes”\\\\n- “Accepted revised beat sheet”\\\\n\\\\n### Safety rule\\\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\\\n\\\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\\\n\\\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\\\n\\\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\\\n\\\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\\\n\\\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\\\n- a curated genre list\\\\n- per-genre tone options\\\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\\\n\\\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\\\n\\\\n## Audio Expectations\\\\n\\\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\\\n\\\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\\\n- Support voice selection\\\\n- Support speed controls\\\\n- Support optional background music\\\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\\\n\\\\n## Export Expectations\\\\n\\\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\\\n- final story text in the app\\\\n- final audio in the app\\\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\\\n- downloadable final audio file\\\\n\\\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\\\n\\\\nFavor:\\\\n- typed interfaces\\\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\\\n- migrations\\\\n- tests around durable state\\\\n- visible logs\\\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\\\n- useful docs\\\\n\\\\nAvoid:\\\\n- stuffing everything into route handlers\\\\n- leaking secrets to the browser\\\\n- relying on browser local state as the source of truth\\\\n- making stage transitions implicit or magical\\\\n- hiding important state in giant untyped JSON blobs when relational structure is obvious\\\\n\\\\n## How to Respond to Each Numbered Prompt\\\\n\\\\nFor each numbered prompt in this pack:\\\\n\\\\n1. Treat it as the next incremental repo task.\\\\n2. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt requires refactor.\\\\n3. Make reasonable local decisions when the prompt leaves room, but stay aligned with this base prompt.\\\\n4. Prefer small, coherent commits worth reviewing.\\\\n5. After implementing, report:\\\\n   - what you changed\\\\n   - key files touched\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:842:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:49:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_25\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' README.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Storyteller\\\\n\\\\nStoryteller is a local-first, full-stack app for creating bedtime stories from first idea to finished text and finished narration audio. The product is session-based: the first meaningful screen is a past-sessions view where a user can resume work in progress, reopen a completed story, or start a new session.\\\\n\\\\nThis repository currently contains the first scaffold for that system. The project charter in this README and the supporting docs define the product and architecture that later prompts will implement.\\\\n\\\\n## Product Vision\\\\n\\\\nThe app should feel like a guided bedtime story studio, not a generic prompt box. A user should be able to:\\\\n\\\\n- See past sessions first and continue where they left off.\\\\n- Collaborate through both chat and direct UI controls.\\\\n- Move through a structured planning workflow before long-form writing starts.\\\\n- Watch story composition and audio generation progress in real time.\\\\n- Return later to read, listen, download, or revise completed work.\\\\n\\\\nThe tone of the product should stay calm, readable, and trustworthy. Wonder, mystery, and adventure are welcome, but bedtime suitability is a hard requirement: emotional stakes should resolve safely and the ending should feel restful.\\\\n\\\\n## Required Story Workflow\\\\n\\\\nThe application is being built around this ordered workflow:\\\\n\\\\n1. Genre selection\\\\n2. Tone selection\\\\n3. Story setup / free-form brief\\\\n4. Story pitches\\\\n5. Character sheet\\\\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\\\n7. Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\\\n8. Composition\\\\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation\\\\n10. Finalize / read / listen / download\\\\n\\\\nEach stage must be durable, resumable, and editable. Chat messages can propose actions in the workflow, and direct UI actions must also be reflected back into the session chat log as compact summaries.\\\\n\\\\n## Fixed Technical Decisions\\\\n\\\\nThese choices are not optional for this project:\\\\n\\\\n- Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript\\\\n- Backend: Python + FastAPI\\\\n- Structured data: PostgreSQL\\\\n- Blob storage: file-backed GCS emulator in local development\\\\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\\\\n- Secrets handling: local `secrets.yaml`, never committed to git\\\\n- AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services\\\\n\\\\nTwo architectural rules follow from those constraints:\\\\n\\\\n- The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.\\\\n- Long-running story composition and narration generation must be durable background jobs with resumable server-side state.\\\\n\\\\nMore detail is in [docs/product-brief.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/product-brief.md) and [docs/architecture-overview.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/architecture-overview.md).\\\\n\\\\n## Local Development Expectations\\\\n\\\\nThe repo is intended to run locally with Docker Compose. At the current scaffold stage:\\\\n\\\\n- `frontend/` contains a Vite React app served on `http://localhost:8566`\\\\n- `backend/` contains a FastAPI app served on `http://localhost:8565`\\\\n- `infra/compose/` holds the canonical Compose file for local orchestration\\\\n- `tools/webapp-qa/` contains the browser automation container used for local UI verification\\\\n\\\\nStart the current stack with:\\\\n\\\\n```bash\\\\n./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe current scaffold does not yet include PostgreSQL or the file-backed GCS emulator, but both are required parts of the target system and will be added through later prompt work. `secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials.\\\\n\\\\n## Repository Shape\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\n.\\\\n├── backend/\\\\n│   ├── app/\\\\n│   │   ├── main.py\\\\n│   │   └── worker/\\\\n│   ├── migrations/\\\\n│   ├── Dockerfile\\\\n│   ├── README.md\\\\n│   └── requirements.txt\\\\n├── docs/\\\\n├── frontend/\\\\n├── infra/\\\\n│   ├── compose/\\\\n│   │   └── docker-compose.yml\\\\n│   └── persistence/\\\\n├── prompts/\\\\n├── scripts/\\\\n├── test-assets/\\\\n└── tools/\\\\n    └── webapp-qa/\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- `frontend/`: browser client and future TypeScript UI modules\\\\n- `backend/`: API code, worker home, and migration home\\\\n- `infra/`: Compose definitions and infrastructure notes\\\\n- `docs/`: product notes, architecture notes, and future ADRs\\\\n- `scripts/`: developer entrypoints such as `dev-compose.sh`\\\\n- `test-assets/`: reusable fixtures for UI, audio, and integration testing\\\\n- `prompts/`: sequential build prompts and task summaries\\\\n- `tools/`: local QA and developer tooling\\\\n\\\\n## What Success Looks Like\\\\n\\\\nA new engineer should be able to open this repository and quickly understand:\\\\n\\\\n- the first screen is the past-sessions home for resume, edit, and create flows\\\\n- the story workflow is staged and ordered\\\\n- the final product writes stories and generates narration audio durably\\\\n- the backend owns AI access, long-running jobs, and persistent state\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:845:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:72:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_37\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/architecture-overview.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Architecture Overview\\\\n\\\\n## System Picture\\\\n\\\\nStoryteller is a full-stack application with a browser client, a backend-owned workflow engine, durable relational state, durable object storage, and resumable background jobs.\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\nReact + Vite client\\\\n        |\\\\n        v\\\\nFastAPI API layer\\\\n        |\\\\n        +--> domain services and policy layer\\\\n        |         |\\\\n        |         +--> AI adapters for planning, composition, and narration\\\\n        |         |\\\\n        |         +--> repositories for sessions, workflow state, jobs, and event log\\\\n        |\\\\n        +--> background workers for composition and audio generation\\\\n                  |\\\\n                  +--> PostgreSQL for durable structured state\\\\n                  +--> file-backed GCS emulator for artifacts and intermediate blobs\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n## Why Gemini Calls Must Stay on the Backend\\\\n\\\\nAll Gemini access belongs on the server side for four reasons:\\\\n\\\\n1. Secrets control: provider credentials live in local `secrets.yaml` and must never enter the browser.\\\\n2. Policy enforcement: chat-to-UI actions and workflow transitions need deterministic server validation before the model can change session state.\\\\n3. Durable orchestration: prompts, structured outputs, retries, and model selection need to be coordinated with persistent session data and job records.\\\\n4. Provider flexibility: keeping Gemini behind backend adapters lets the app keep a stable internal interface while model IDs, structured output tactics, or narration providers evolve.\\\\n\\\\nThe frontend should only call the backend's API and subscribe to backend-owned progress events.\\\\n\\\\n## Why Composition and Audio Must Use Resumable Server-Side Jobs\\\\n\\\\nComposition and narration are long-running operations that cannot safely live inside request threads or browser memory. They need server-side jobs because:\\\\n\\\\n- a user must be able to refresh the page or return later without losing progress\\\\n- partial story text and audio segments need durable checkpoints\\\\n- progress updates need a single source of truth that both chat and UI can observe\\\\n- retries, cancellation, and resume logic need explicit job state rather than transient in-memory state\\\\n\\\\nThe durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after interruption.\\\\n\\\\n## Intended Backend Shape\\\\n\\\\nThe target backend should separate concerns explicitly:\\\\n\\\\n- `api/`: HTTP routes and websocket endpoints\\\\n- `settings/`: environment and secrets loading\\\\n- `db/`: database engine, migrations, and persistence setup\\\\n- `models/`: typed domain models and persistence schemas\\\\n- `repositories/`: database access for sessions, events, jobs, and assets\\\\n- `services/`: workflow logic and policy enforcement\\\\n- `ai/`: Gemini planning, composition, and narration adapters\\\\n- `worker/`: background job execution for writing and audio\\\\n- `storage/`: object storage abstraction over the local GCS emulator\\\\n\\\\n## Durable Domain Concepts\\\\n\\\\nThe system should persist the following concepts as first-class records:\\\\n\\\\n- Session\\\\n- Workflow stage\\\\n- Event log\\\\n- Selected genre\\\\n- Selected tone\\\\n- Story brief\\\\n- Pitch batch and selected pitch\\\\n- Character batch and selected character sheet\\\\n- Beat sheet\\\\n- Story setup preferences\\\\n- Outline or chapter plan\\\\n- Continuity bible\\\\n- Composition job and segments\\\\n- Audio job and segments\\\\n- Asset metadata\\\\n\\\\n## Current Repository State\\\\n\\\\nThe repository currently contains an initial scaffold:\\\\n\\\\n- `frontend/`: Vite React app\\\\n- `backend/`: FastAPI app\\\\n- `backend/migrations/`: reserved home for database schema history\\\\n- `backend/app/worker/`: reserved home for background job runners\\\\n- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`: local frontend, backend, and browser QA services\\\\n- `infra/persistence/`: notes for persistent local infrastructure data\\\\n- `scripts/dev-compose.sh`: convenience entrypoint for the Compose stack\\\\n- `tools/webapp-qa/`: containerized browser automation support\\\\n\\\\nPostgreSQL, the file-backed GCS emulator, background workers, and most domain layers are still planned work. This document records the required direction so later prompts can extend the scaffold without changing the architectural contract.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:891:    DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = CORSMiddleware\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1084:            CORSMiddleware,\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1145:        assert settings.gcs_bucket_name == \\\"storyteller-dev\\\"\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1460:    - AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1464:    - The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini {settings.gcs_endpoint}.\\\"\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:2179:    backend/app/settings/config.py:9:DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = The main design choice left is whether to keep a light custom loader or move to a stricter typed validator; I’m checking later storage prompts to choose names that won’t fight upcoming bucket work.\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2318:    prompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:1737:    prompts/06-gitignore-and-secrets-hygiene.yolopilot.md:1507:    The tone of the product should stay calm, readable, and trustworthy. Wonder, mystery, and adventure are welcome, but bedtime suitability is a hard requirement: emotional stakes should resolve safely and the ending should feel restful.\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2319:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:421:    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:14:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_5\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` — React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` — FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` — Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` — architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\n\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\n\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\n\\n### Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- “Selected genre: Quest Fantasy”\\n- “Updated target runtime: ~12 minutes”\\n- “Accepted revised beat sheet”\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2323:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:450:    prompts/04-docker-compose-foundation.codex.jsonlines:42:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_21\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2326:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:544:    prompts/04-docker-compose-foundation.yolopilot.jsonlines:45:{\"color\": \"cyan\", \"emoji\": \"\\ud83d\\udee0\\ufe0f\", \"event_type\": \"Command Finished\", \"message\": \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# Base Prompt \\u2014 Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` \\u2014 React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` \\u2014 FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` \\u2014 Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` \\u2014 architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\n\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\n\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\n\\n### Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- \\u201cSelected genre: Quest Fantasy\\u201d\\n- \\u201cUpdated target runtime: ~12 minutes\\u201d\\n- \\u201cAccepted revised beat sheet\\u201d\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\", \"exit_code\": 0, \"id\": \"item_21\", \"status\": \"completed\", \"type\": \"command_execution\"}, \"type\": \"item.completed\"}, \"task_display_context\": {\"event_number\": 45, \"prompt_index\": 5, \"prompt_name\": \"04 \\u2014 Docker Compose Foundation\", \"prompt_total\": 100}, \"timestap\": \"2026-03-31T21:31:59-07:00\"}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2328:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:621:    prompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines:17:{\"color\": \"cyan\", \"emoji\": \"\\ud83d\\udee0\\ufe0f\", \"event_type\": \"Command Finished\", \"message\": \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# Base Prompt \\u2014 Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` \\u2014 React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` \\u2014 FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` \\u2014 Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` \\u2014 architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- \\u201cSelected genre: Quest Fantasy\\u201d\\n- \\u201cUpdated target runtime: ~12 minutes\\u201d\\n- \\u201cAccepted revised beat sheet\\u201d\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\", \"exit_code\": 0, \"id\": \"item_5\", \"status\": \"completed\", \"type\": \"command_execution\"}, \"type\": \"item.completed\"}, \"task_display_context\": {\"event_number\": 17, \"prompt_index\": 1, \"prompt_name\": \"00 \\u2014 Project Charter and Success Criteria\", \"prompt_total\": 100}, \"timestap\": \"2026-03-31T20:56:08-07:00\"}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2332:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:839:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:22:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_10\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n- useful docs\\n\\nAvoid:\\n- stuffing everything into route handlers\\n- leaking secrets to the browser\\n- relying on browser local state as the source of truth\\n- making stage transitions implicit or magical\\n- hiding important state in giant untyped JSON blobs when relational structure is obvious\\n\\n## How to Respond to Each Numbered Prompt\\n\\nFor each numbered prompt in this pack:\\n\\n1. Treat it as the next incremental repo task.\\n2. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt requires refactor.\\n3. Make reasonable local decisions when the prompt leaves room, but stay aligned with this base prompt.\\n4. Prefer small, coherent commits worth reviewing.\\n5. After implementing, report:\\n   - what you changed\\n   - key files touched\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2335:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:2296:    prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:14:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_5\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` — React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` — FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` — Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` — architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\n\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\n\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\n\\n### Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- “Selected genre: Quest Fantasy”\\n- “Updated target runtime: ~12 minutes”\\n- “Accepted revised beat sheet”\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.yolopilot.md:2339:    prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:2310:    prompts/04-docker-compose-foundation.codex.jsonlines:42:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_21\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**.
